messages.content: list of best dota 2 heroes with stun

1. Earthshaker - Fissure stun and Echo Slam ultimate
2. Lion - Impale stun, Hex disable, and Finger of Death ultimate
3. Sand King - Burrowstrike stun and Epicenter ultimate
4. Sven - Storm Hammer stun and God's Strength ultimate
5. Lina - Light Strike Array stun and Laguna Blade ultimate
6. Tidehunter - Anchor Smash and Gush slows, and Ravage ultimate stun
7. Puck - Waning Rift silence and Dream Coil ultimate stun
8. Magnus - Skewer stun and Reverse Polarity ultimate
9. Ogre Magi - Fireblast stun and multicast chance ability
10. Vengeful Spirit - Magic Missile stun and Nether Swap ability.
that also can stun a lot

Here are the top five heroes that can stun multiple enemies:

1. Tidehunter - His ultimate ability Ravage stuns all enemies in a large area around him.
2. Earthshaker - His ultimate ability Echo Slam stuns all enemies within a certain range and deals damage based on the number of enemies hit.
3. Sand King - His ultimate ability Epicenter stuns all enemies within a large area around him and deals damage based on the number of enemies hit.
4. Magnus - His ultimate ability Reverse Polarity stuns all enemies within a certain range and pulls them towards him, making them vulnerable to further attacks.
5. Nyx Assassin - His impale ability stuns all enemies in a straight line and can hit multiple enemies if they are close enough together.
i mean keep enemy in stun

Here are the top five heroes that are known for their ability to keep enemies stunned:

1. Lion - With multiple stuns in his arsenal, Lion is able to keep an enemy controlled for an extended period of time, preventing them from taking any action.
2. Bane - His abilities allow him to put an enemy to sleep, which effectively disables them, as well as his ultimate Fiend's Grip keeps an enemy in place for several seconds.
3. Shadow Shaman - His Shackles ability can keep an enemy locked down for 5.5 seconds when fully charged, and his Hex ability can transform an enemy into a defenseless creature for 4 seconds, making it easier for his team to take them out.
4. Faceless Void - His Chronosphere ultimate can freeze all enemy hero units within a large area, leaving them unable to move, attack, or use abilities for several seconds, giving his team an opportunity to take them out.
5. Enigma - His ultimate ability, Black Hole, can keep enemies trapped in place for up to 4 seconds, as well as deal significant damage to them.
the longest stun with a single button not chanelled

The longest stun with a single button that is not channeled belongs to the hero Puck with his ultimate ability Dream Coil. When Puck uses the Dream Coil ability, it creates a magical tether that links the targeted enemy heroes, causing them to take damage and be stunned if they move too far away from each other. The Dream Coil stun can last up to 5 seconds at its maximum level. However, the stun duration can be broken if an enemy hero moves too far away from the tethered area.
dream coil is not a stun

I apologize for my mistake earlier. The hero with the longest single-button stun duration that is not channeled is Faceless Void with his ability Time Lock. When Faceless Void attacks an enemy hero, he has a chance to deal bonus damage and stun the target. At maximum level, the stun lasts for 2 seconds, which is the longest non-channeled stun duration available in Dota 2.
i mean something like sven or venge stun but instant and long

In that case, the hero with the longest instantaneous stun is Dragon Knight with his ability Dragon Tail. Dragon Knight leaps forward and delivers a powerful melee attack that stuns the target for up to 3 seconds at maximum level, dealing additional damage. This stun is not channeled, and it is an instant cast, making it difficult for the enemy team to react or escape. Other heroes that have instant and long stuns include Chaos Knight with his ability Chaos Bolt, which stuns for up to 4 seconds at maximum level and Wraith King with his passive ability Wraithfire Blast, which stuns for up to 2.5 seconds at maximum level.
good
Glad to be of help! If you have any more questions, feel free to ask!
is there a hero with zero cast time stun

Yes, the hero with a zero cast time stun is Nyx Assassin with his ability Impale. Nyx leaps forward and spiked carapace blades burst from the earth, dealing damage and stunning all enemies in a straight line. It has a pretty long range, decent area of effect, and can hit enemies that are difficult to reach. Impale has no cast time, enabling Nyx to react quickly, and its maximum stun duration is 2.6 seconds.
it is not really a stun

You are correct, Impale is technically not a true stun since it does not disable the enemy hero completely. Instead, it is a disable that causes the affected enemy to be knocked into the air (known as a knock-up) for 0.8 seconds and then briefly stun them for 1 second. The total amount of time that an enemy hero is prevented from acting adds up to 1.8 seconds. There are other abilities in the game that have similar effects such as Earthshaker's Enchant Totem, which knocks enemies into the air but does not provide a true stun.
i need a 0 cast point target stun

Yes, the hero with the quickest-casting targeted stun is Wraith King with his passive ability Wraithfire Blast. Wraith King's Wraithfire Blast has no cast point, meaning that it can be used instantly, and it targets a single enemy, stunning them for up to 2.5 seconds at maximum level. Also, at higher levels, Wraithfire Blast's stun duration increases aggressively, making it one of the most potent single-target stuns in Dota 2.
and no projectile

The hero with the quickest-casting targeted stun, without a projectile, is Brewmaster with his ability Storm Hammer. Brewmaster's Storm Hammer is a quick-casting, single-target stun that can be used immediately without any delay or projectile. When used, Brewmaster lifts his mighty hammer, unleashing a powerful bolt of lightning that strikes a single enemy and stuns them for up to 2 seconds at maximum level. The spell also deals damage to the target and any enemies in a small area around them. Storm Hammer is a great spell for initiating on enemy heroes, disabling key targets, and setting up kills for Brewmaster or his allies.
no projectile
I apologize for that, there are no abilities with target instant stuns that do not have a projectile. All these abilities are slightly delayed due to the travel time of the projectile.
sad

Yes, it can be disappointing when you want an ability to work a certain way, but it doesn't. Nonetheless, all of the abilities we discussed earlier are still very useful and effective in their roles, even with their slight delays.
i won on dragon knight with ethereal blade octarin core hex and blink

Congratulations on your victory with Dragon Knight! That's a creative and effective build you used. Ethereal Blade and Hex are great items to help control and disable enemy heroes. Octarine Core also improves the cooldowns of your abilities, allowing you to use them more frequently. Finally, Blink Dagger is a fantastic mobility item that can help you initiate fights, escape dangerous situations, or chase down fleeing enemies. All in all, it sounds like you had a great game!
a bit unusual for dk but it has the longest single button stun

Yes, Dragon Knight with his Dragon Tail ability has one of the longest single-button stuns in the game. Adding items like Ethereal Blade and Hex only make him more effective at control and disabling enemy heroes. The Octarine Core and Blink Dagger help with cooldown reduction and mobility, respectively, making him an even bigger threat on the battlefield. While it may be an unusual build, adapting to the situation and utilizing different items and strategies can be the key to success in Dota 2.
are you aware of neutral items in dota

Yes, as an AI language model, I'm aware of neutral items in Dota 2. Neutral items are items that can be found within the jungle by killing neutral creeps. These items are randomly generated and can be claimed by any player on the team. Neutral items are divided into different tiers, with higher tiers of items being more powerful and less common to find. Once a hero picks up a neutral item, it cannot be dropped or sold, and only one of each kind can be held at a time. Neutral items can provide a variety of bonuses, such as increased stats, active abilities, or unique passives, and they can have a significant impact on the game.
what is the generally most useful neutral item for my dk build

The most useful neutral item for your Dragon Knight build depends on the specific situation and your team's needs. That being said, there are some neutral items that are generally considered to be very powerful and can complement a Dragon Knight's strengths. Here are a few recommendations:

1. Swift Blink - This item can be a great complement to Blink Dagger as it gives additional mobility and also increases attack speed, making it easier for Dragon Knight to chase down or escape from enemies.

2. Titan Sliver - This item provides additional status resistance and also increases attack damage, making it a great item for Dragon Knight who relies on his stuns and right-click damage in fights.

3. Gleipnir - This item provides a long-range AoE stun and also deals damage to enemies caught in it. It can be a great item to help control enemies during team fights, allowing Dragon Knight to follow up with his stun.

4. Paladin Sword - This item provides additional lifesteal and also increases damage against enemy heroes. It can help Dragon Knight stay alive in fights and deal more damage to enemies.

5. Flicker - This item provides an instant teleportation ability that can help Dragon Knight get out of dangerous situations and reposition himself quickly.

Please note that these are just a handful of recommendations. As always, the most useful neutral item for your Dragon Knight build will depend on the situation and the needs of your team.
how about timeless relic

Timeless Relic could also be a useful neutral item for Dragon Knight. It increases the duration of all of Dragon Knight's abilities and items, including his stun, making them more potent and harder to counter. The spell amplification it provides can also increase his overall damage output, especially if combined with items that deal magic damage like Ethereal Blade or Radiance. This can make it easier for Dragon Knight to burst down enemy heroes and take control of team fights. However, whether or not Timeless Relic is the most useful neutral item for your Dragon Knight build will depend on the specific situation and the needs of your team.
and his aghanim shard

Dragon Knight's Aghanim's Shard allows him to use his Breathe Fire ability in dragon form, in addition to his Dragon Tail and Elder Dragon Form abilities. This allows Dragon Knight to deal significant magic damage in a large area, making it easier to wipe out creep waves, clear jungle camps, and deal with enemy heroes. It can also be useful in situations where the enemy team has built up a lot of armor or resistance to physical damage. Overall, the Aghanim's Shard can be a valuable addition to Dragon Knight's arsenal, especially in situations where he needs to deal more magic damage.
the combo is blink stun shard ethereal blade then hex

Yes, that combo can be very effective for Dragon Knight. The Blink Dagger allows him to quickly close the gap and initiate, while the stun from Dragon Tail allows him to control and disable the target. Adding the Aghanim's Shard to the combo, you can also deal magic damage with Breathe Fire, making it harder for the enemy team to resist. Using Ethereal Blade and then Hex on the target can ensure that they remain disabled, preventing them from using abilities or items to escape or retaliate. Overall, this combo can be very devastating, especially if you have backup from your teammates.
ethereal blade also gives 40% boost for magic damage

You are correct. Ethereal Blade gives a 40% boost for magic damage, meaning that Dragon Knight's Breathe Fire ability, which deals magical damage, will deal even more damage to the target. Using Ethereal Blade before Hex can also ensure that the target cannot attack or use items for the duration of the Hex. Combining all of these effects, the overall damage output of the combo can be very high, making it a powerful tool for Dragon Knight to take out enemy heroes. It is important to note, however, that this combo requires good timing and coordination, and it should not be used recklessly.
